metaTitle: Seoul Subway Lost & Found — recovery service | LFK
metaDescription: Lost an item on the Seoul subway? Each line has a lost-and-found office. We coordinate in Korean and ship items worldwide.
h1: Lost something on the Seoul subway
heroBody: Seoul's subway is one of the most extensive in the world, with nine main lines and a dedicated lost-and-found system per line. Items are collected by station staff and either held at the line's central office or routed to Lost112 (the national lost-and-found database). Each line has its own contact number and pickup hours.

steps.title: Tell us the line and approximate time
steps.body: Submit a free report with: the line number (1, 2, 3, etc.), nearest station, approximate time, and a description. Even one piece of that info helps narrow the search.

steps.title: We contact the line's lost-and-found office
steps.body: Each Seoul subway line operates its own lost-and-found office (usually at a central station like Sindorim, Wangsimni, or City Hall). We call in Korean and check whether your item has been logged.

steps.title: Cross-check Lost112
steps.body: If the line's office doesn't have it, we check Lost112 — the national database where transit operators upload found items. Subway items often surface there within 24–48 hours of being turned in.

steps.title: Retrieve and ship
steps.body: Once located, we collect the item, package it, and ship via EMS or express courier to your address worldwide.

faqs.a: If you remember the line color (orange = Line 3, green = Line 2, etc.), that's enough. If you remember the station, we can identify the line from there. If you remember neither, just the approximate area of Seoul, we'll cross-check the most likely lines.

faqs.q: How long do subway lost-and-founds keep items?
faqs.a: About 7 days at the line's office, then transfer to Lost112's central police storage for up to 9 months for valuable items. Earlier is easier.

faqs.q: What's the recovery rate for subway losses?
faqs.a: High for distinctive items (laptops, named bags) — Korean station staff are diligent about turning items in. Lower for generic items like a black wallet without ID, just because matching is harder.

faqs.q: Can you handle Airport Railroad (AREX) or KTX?
faqs.a: Yes — AREX and KTX (Korea's high-speed rail) have their own separate lost-and-found channels. We work all of them based on which train you took.
